heading: Army industrial facilities: cooperative activities with non-Army entities
label: 7544 Army industrial facilities: cooperative activities with non-Army entities
notes: Prior ProvisionsPrior sections 7544 to 7547 were renumbered sections 8764 to 8767 of this title, respectively.

children.heading: .—Cooperative Arrangements Authorized
children.label: a .—Cooperative Arrangements Authorized
children.text: A working-capital funded Army industrial facility may enter into a contract or other cooperative arrangement with a non-Army entity to carry out with the non-Army entity a military or commercial project described in subsection (b), subject to the conditions prescribed in subsection (c).
The sale of articles manufactured by the facility or services performed by the facility to persons outside the Department of the Army.
The performance of work by a non-Army entity at the facility.
The performance of work by the facility for a non-Army entity.
The sharing of work by the facility and a non-Army entity.
The leasing, or use under a facilities use contract or otherwise, of the facility (including excess capacity) or equipment (including excess equipment) of the facility by a non-Army entity.
The preparation and submission of joint offers by the facility and a non-Army entity for competitive procurements entered into with Federal agency.

In the case of an article to be manufactured or services to be performed by the facility, the articles can be substantially manufactured, or the services can be substantially performed, by the facility without subcontracting for more than incidental performance.

children.label: A
children.text: work by the facility for the Department of Defense; or

children.label: B
children.text: a military mission of the facility.

children.label: A
children.text: Maximized utilization of the capacity of the facility.

children.label: B
children.text: Reduction or elimination of the cost of ownership of the facility.

children.label: C
children.text: Reduction in the cost of manufacturing or maintaining Department of Defense products at the facility.

children.label: D
children.text: Preservation of skills or equipment related to a core competency of the facility.

children.label: A
children.text: in any case of willful misconduct or gross negligence; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a claim by a purchaser of articles or services under this section that damages or injury arose from the failure of the United States to comply with quality, schedule, or cost performance requirements in the contract to carry out the activity.
enter into a firm, fixed-price contract (or, if agreed to by the non-Army entity, a cost reimbursement contract) for a sale of articles or services or use of equipment or facilities;
enter into a multiyear contract for a period not to exceed five years, unless a longer period is specifically authorized by law;

charge the non-Army entity the amounts necessary to recover the full costs of the articles or services provided, including capital improvement costs, and equipment depreciation costs associated with providing the articles, services, equipment, or facilities;
authorize the non-Army entity to use incremental funding to pay for the articles, services, or use of equipment or facilities; and
accept payment-in-kind.

children.heading: .—Proceeds Credited to Working Capital Fund
children.label: e .—Proceeds Credited to Working Capital Fund
children.text: The proceeds received from the sale of an article or service pursuant to a contract or other cooperative arrangement under this section shall be credited to the working capital fund that incurs the cost of manufacturing the article or performing the service.

children.heading: .—Approval Authority
children.label: f .—Approval Authority
children.text: The authority of an Army industrial facility to enter into a cooperative arrangement under subsection (a) shall be exercised at the level of the commander of the major subordinate command of the Army that has responsibility for the facility. The commander may approve such an arrangement on a case-by-case basis or a class basis.

children.heading: .—Commercial Sales
children.label: g .—Commercial Sales
children.text: Except in the case of work performed for the Department of Defense, for a contract of the Department of Defense, for foreign military sales, or for authorized foreign direct commercial sales (defense articles or defense services sold to a foreign government or international organization under export controls), a sale of articles or services may be made under this section only if the approval authority described in subsection (f) determines that the articles or services are not available from a commercial source located in the United States in the required quantity or quality, or within the time required.

children.heading: .—Exclusion From Depot-Level Maintenance and Repair Percentage Limitation
children.label: h .—Exclusion From Depot-Level Maintenance and Repair Percentage Limitation
children.text: Amounts expended for the performance of a depot-level maintenance and repair workload by non-Federal Government personnel at an Army industrial facility shall not be counted for purposes of applying the percentage limitation in if the personnel are provided by a non-Army entity pursuant to a cooperative arrangement entered into under subsection (a).section 2466(a) of this title

foreign military sales and the export controls provided for in sections 30 and 38 of the Arms Export Control Act ( and 2778) to activities of a cooperative arrangement entered into under subsection (a); and22 U.S.C. 2770
to leases of non-excess property in the administration of such an arrangement.section 2667 of this title
The term “Army industrial facility” includes an ammunition plant, an arsenal, a depot, and a manufacturing plant.

children.label: A
children.text: A Federal agency (other than the Department of the Army).

children.label: B
children.text: An entity in industry or commercial sales.

children.label: C
children.text: A State or political subdivision of a State.

children.label: D
children.text: An institution of higher education or vocational training institution.

children.label: A
children.text: are made as the work on manufacture or articles is being performed or services are being performed or equipment or facilities are used, as the case may be; and

children.label: B
children.text: result in full payment being completed as the required work is being completed.

The term “full costs”, with respect to articles or services provided under a cooperative arrangement entered into under subsection (a), means the variable costs and the fixed costs that are directly related to the production of the articles or the provision of the services.
The term “variable costs” means the costs that are expected to fluctuate directly with the volume of sales or services provided or the use of equipment or facilities.
